evals.name: banners-and-doc-refs
evals.expected_output: Flags every section-divider banner (empty or titled, ===== and -----) as R5 REMOVE, and every internal doc reference (DD_ARCH.md, DD_PLAN.md) as R4. Keeps the external RFC reference and the genuine WHY rationale.
assertions: Flags top '===== Datadog =====' banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- Provider credentials -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- DD GCP integration ... -----' titled banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '# ----- Metric namespace allowlist -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags 'DD_ARCH.md §3.2' under R4
Flags 'DD_PLAN.md T4.1' under R4 (keep rationale, drop ref)
Keeps 'RFC 9110 §10.2.4' (R4 external exception)
Keeps metric-allowlist WHY rationale

evals.name: file-refs-and-diagram
evals.expected_output: Flags the source-file references and dividers, keeps the ASCII state-machine diagram, the decision comment, and the external CVE reference.
assertions: Flags 'docs/scheduling.md' + 'utils/backoff.ts' under R4
Flags '// ----- helpers -----' as R5 REMOVE
Flags top '===== Scheduler =====' banner as R5 REMOVE
Flags '// increment the attempt counter' as R1 REMOVE
Keeps ASCII state-machine diagram (R5 exception)
Keeps 'Sequential, not parallel ...' decision comment (R2)
Keeps 'CVE-2024-1234' external reference (R4 exception)

evals.name: misplaced-and-duplicated-rationale
evals.expected_output: Flags the behavioral rationale parked on the enum log-code as R12: MOVE when it only lives on the declaration, REMOVE when the same reason is already present at the handler that performs the conditional UPDATE. Keeps a value-meaning note on a declaration (R12 contrast) and a genuine WHY that already sits at its method.
assertions: Flags '// the conditional UPDATE can't distinguish a missing row from an ineligible status' on the enum member as R12
Verdict is REMOVE when the same rationale already exists at the handler's UPDATE (duplicated)
Verdict is MOVE (naming the handler method) when the rationale exists only on the enum declaration
Keeps '// 0 means unbounded, not disabled' value-meaning note on a constant (R12 contrast)
Keeps the genuine WHY already located at the handler method (R2)
Does not flag bare enum members that carry no comment

evals.name: spec-id-references
evals.expected_output: Treats bare spec/requirement-ID tokens (F1, Q1, §4.1, R7, AC-3/AC-4) as R4 internal-doc references even without a filename: REMOVE the pure pointers (F1 narration, 'see Q1', 'Implements AC-3 and AC-4'), REWRITE the ones that carry a real constraint by stripping the token and keeping only the load-bearing sentence (§4.1 cap, R7 ordering rule). Crucially, no kept/rewritten comment retains the token. Keeps the external RFC pin, the genuine WHY, and the value-meaning note where 'R2' is a real string literal the code uses.
assertions: Flags '// F1: users can submit a payment with a saved card' as R4 with verdict REMOVE
Flags '// see Q1' as R4 REMOVE (pure pointer, nothing survives the strip)
Flags '// §4.1 caps a single charge at 500_000 minor units' as R4 REWRITE
The §4.1 suggested fix does NOT contain the token '§4.1' or '4.1'
Flags '// R7: charge in source order ...' as R4 REWRITE and keeps the ordering rationale
The R7 suggested fix does NOT contain the token 'R7' but keeps 'out-of-order' / 'source order'
Flags '/** Implements AC-3 and AC-4. */' as R4 REMOVE (pure pointer doc)
Keeps '// Retry-After handling per RFC 9110 §10.2.4' (R4 external-pin exception)
Keeps '// region R2 is EU-only; never route US tenants here' (R2 is a real string literal, value-meaning note)
Keeps '// sequential, not parallel — the gateway rate-limits per merchant IP' (genuine WHY)
No kept or rewritten comment text in the report retains any spec-id fragment (F1, Q1, AC-3, AC-4, §4.1, R7)

evals.name: spec-id-embedded-midsentence
evals.expected_output: Spec/requirement-ID tokens woven mid-sentence into otherwise genuine WHY comments (R2, F3, Q4, §2.3, §2.4) are the hard case: the comment is worth keeping but the token must not ride along. Each of the three real-WHY comments is REWRITE with the token scrubbed and the rationale (silent fail-open, revoked-token window, validation ordering) preserved. The bare R1 narration line is REMOVE. Reasoning must check whether a token is a real code value before stripping rather than rationalizing it as one.
assertions: The lines 4-6 comment is REWRITE, scrubbing both 'R2' and 'F3' while keeping the silent-fail-open rationale
The lines 11-12 comment is REWRITE, scrubbing 'Per Q4' while keeping the revoked-token constraint
The lines 17-18 comment is REWRITE, scrubbing 'see §2.3 then §2.4' while keeping the shape-before-semantics ordering rationale
No rewritten comment retains R2, F3, Q4, §2.3 or §2.4
The bare R1 line '// hosts are matched case-insensitively' is REMOVE
Reasoning does not rationalize a prose-only token as a real code value
